name: ETH Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
edge_mechanism: inventory-reset continuation after flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner lane for testing whether a flush below fast value that quickly reclaims balance can produce a continuation move as trapped inventory gets repaired. The edge is the reclaim after the flush, not a naive oversold bounce.
invalidation_condition: Abort if flush-and-reclaim events remain too sparse, if reclaimed moves cannot extend after costs, or if the mechanism just creates weak dead-cat bounces.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below short-term value, reclaims balance quickly, confirms directional handoff, and then continues as the inventory reset resolves.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition tapes frequently trap late participants on the flush, then repair sharply once value is reclaimed. ETH is the best clean lane to test that repair-continuation sequence.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is appropriate because flush-and-reclaim structure is readable and should remain dense enough without altcoin noise dominating the signal.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Reclaims may occur but fail to travel far enough after entry, leaving the setup as a low-energy mean-reversion bounce instead of real continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trigger with acceptable density but follow-through is weak, simplify exit ambition and front-load profit-taking before narrowing the reclaim conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A decent number of repair-continuation trades with front-loaded exits carrying most expectancy and occasional secondary extension.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h plus one cross-lane show real density and positive post-cost continuation after flush-and-reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated reclaims that stall immediately after entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; flush, reclaim, and directional handoff are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim logic rather than ceremonial exact-bar failure patterns

test_lanes.why_included: Cleanest lane for repair-continuation after a flush and reclaim.
test_lanes.continue_if: Reclaim entries show usable density and positive follow-through after costs.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Reclaims occur but do not extend enough or become too sparse.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A visible set of flush-reclaim-repair continuation trades.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same inventory-reset grammar survives on a noisier lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: DOGE preserves the reclaim shape with acceptable density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: DOGE becomes too chaotic or anti-correlated.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: More frequent but noisier reclaim continuations than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same repair grammar survives on the most expressive lane in the basket.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO still shows usable reclaim continuation after the flush.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The pattern becomes too violent or too sparse.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger repair continuations than ETH.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_55 or 4 bars
